title: The Susie

jokes.text: Hey, if I were a gigolo, how much do you think I could charge a night?
jokes.setup: Kramer casually asking about hypothetical gigolo rates
jokes.punchline: The matter-of-fact delivery of an absurd career consideration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer - treats ridiculous career idea as serious business consideration

jokes.text: Here or in Japan? / What's the difference? / Well, the Japanese are more enlightened. They can see beyond the physical.
jokes.setup: Jerry's practical question about location pricing
jokes.punchline: Kramer's pseudo-philosophical explanation about Japanese enlightenment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from practical to absurdly philosophical - classic Kramer logic

jokes.text: Forget Japan. How much would you pay? / Oh, I don't know. A dollar.
jokes.setup: Kramer asking Jerry to value his services
jokes.punchline: Jerry's brutally low assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery - Jerry's casual cruelty is economical and devastating

jokes.text: I think I'm worth at least 300.
jokes.punchline: Kramer's delusionally high counter-offer
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Kramer's obliviousness to Jerry's insult

jokes.text: You're demeaning me. / You're a gigolo. / Well, you hired me. I'm the victim here.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's matter-of-fact response and Kramer's victimization claim
jokes.explanation: Nice role-playing absurdity with Kramer claiming victimhood

jokes.text: When does it start? / Don't know. They tell you the night before.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's absurd claim about last-minute notification
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational humor about the arbitrary nature of time changes

jokes.text: I am springing ahead right now.
jokes.punchline: Kramer's unilateral decision to change time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer - taking individual action against societal conventions

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sure that won't cause any problems.
jokes.setup: Kramer's plan to individually change time
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sarcastic understatement
jokes.explanation: Solid straight-man response but fairly predictable sarcasm

jokes.text: Don't tell me you're still mad at him for calling you a phony.
jokes.punchline: Reference to five-year-old grudge over 'phony' comment
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Jerry's petty grudge-holding nature

jokes.text: Fine. And I'm not just saying that.
jokes.setup: Mike asking how things are going
jokes.punchline: Jerry's over-sincere clarification reveals his phoniness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware moment where Jerry proves the 'phony' accusation by trying to disprove it

jokes.text: No openings in arson?
jokes.setup: Mike announcing he's become a bookie
jokes.punchline: Jerry's suggestion of more serious crime
jokes.explanation: Sharp, dark one-liner with perfect economy and unexpected escalation

jokes.text: No, no, no. No bets for me. I got a disease.
jokes.punchline: Kramer treating gambling addiction like medical condition
jokes.explanation: Good Kramer characterization - medicalizing behavioral issues

jokes.text: It's all water near a bridge.
jokes.setup: Mike asking if Jerry's still mad
jokes.punchline: Kramer's malapropism of 'water under the bridge'
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer malapropism - close enough to be recognizable but wrong enough to be funny

jokes.text: I don't see you as a Susie. Sharon, maybe.
jokes.setup: Elaine being called Susie at work
jokes.punchline: Jerry's alternative name suggestion
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about name associations

jokes.text: What am I, a bulimic, chain-smoking stenographer from Staten Island?
jokes.setup: Jerry suggesting she looks like a Sharon
jokes.punchline: Elaine's highly specific stereotype escalation
jokes.explanation: Great specific comedy - the alliteration and specificity make it memorable

jokes.text: Kind of a Yankee prom?
jokes.punchline: Jerry reducing fancy event to high school dance
jokes.explanation: Good deflation humor - reducing pretentious event to mundane equivalent

jokes.text: This woman is genetically engineered to go to a ball.
jokes.punchline: Absurd genetic engineering metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good George hyperbole - treating dating strategy like science

jokes.text: Tall, blond, lithe. / Live? / Lithe. / Live? / Lithe. / Oh, lithe.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's repeated mishearing creates confusion
jokes.explanation: Basic wordplay confusion - gets mild laughs but nothing special

jokes.text: Backless? You gonna back her in?
jokes.punchline: Jerry's literal interpretation for entry method
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with good economy but predictable setup

jokes.text: She's not gonna twi... / She'll twirl.
jokes.punchline: George's absolute certainty about twirling
jokes.explanation: Good George obsessiveness about social protocols

jokes.text: Is that the best you could do?
jokes.punchline: George complaining about premium seats
jokes.explanation: Perfect George ingratitude - complaining about incredible gift

jokes.text: She doesn't want to. She needs to talk. / Nobody needs to talk. Who would want to?
jokes.setup: George explaining Allison's desire to talk
jokes.punchline: Jerry's philosophy about relationship conversations
jokes.explanation: Great relationship observation - universal truth about dreaded conversations

jokes.text: I told her I was out of soda. I went out to get some, and I never went back.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking what George did when she tried to break up
jokes.punchline: George's elaborate avoidance scheme
jokes.explanation: Classic George cowardice - elaborate scheme to avoid confrontation is both pathetic and brilliant

jokes.text: And she wants to break up with you. / Can you believe it?
jokes.setup: Jerry stating the obvious about Allison
jokes.punchline: George's genuine shock at predictable outcome
jokes.explanation: Good George obliviousness - can't see how his behavior causes problems

jokes.text: Everybody breaks up at Pomodoros.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's matter-of-fact assessment of restaurant's breakup reputation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational absurdity - restaurant specializing in breakups is perfectly Seinfeld

jokes.text: No, I like the ball. This is my one chance to make a great entrance.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking if George really likes Allison
jokes.punchline: George's honest admission about shallow motivation
jokes.explanation: Perfect George shallowness - admits to using girlfriend for social status

jokes.text: You've made some fine exits.
jokes.setup: George lamenting never making great entrances
jokes.punchline: Jerry's backhanded compliment about George's departures
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to George's dramatic departures with perfect economy

jokes.text: If she can't find me, she can't break up with me. And if we're still going out, she has to go to the ball.
jokes.punchline: George's flawed logic about relationship status
jokes.explanation: Brilliant George logic - treating breakups like legal technicalities

jokes.text: That's some sweet action. / But I don't want any sweet action.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's rejection of Kramer's enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Kramer's excitement and Jerry's reluctance

jokes.text: Well, I couldn't do it. I got a gambling problem. So you put down my money? You don't have a problem.
jokes.setup: Kramer explaining why he bet Jerry's money
jokes.punchline: Twisted logic about gambling addiction by proxy
jokes.explanation: Great Kramer logic - solving his problem by creating Jerry's problem

jokes.text: You know, it's amazing Peterman hasn't fired that dolt.
jokes.setup: Peggy talking to Elaine about Elaine's work
jokes.punchline: Insulting someone to their face unknowingly
jokes.explanation: Classic dramatic irony - audience knows what character doesn't

jokes.text: It's not Suz, all right? It's Susie. My name is Susie.
jokes.punchline: Elaine's firm correction establishing fake identity
jokes.explanation: Good setup for ongoing identity confusion plot

jokes.text: Believe it or not George isn't at home / Please leave a message At the beep
jokes.punchline: Elaborate song message based on TV theme
jokes.explanation: Iconic George moment - perfectly captures his self-importance and pop culture obsession

jokes.text: No, I can't. She knows I go there. It's not secure.
jokes.punchline: George treating relationship like espionage operation
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of George's avoidance into military terminology

jokes.text: I hate that stupid message.
jokes.punchline: Allison's blunt assessment interrupting the song
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of George's pretension with economy

jokes.text: Oh, I guess you're not at home. I probably should have tried you at the office.
jokes.setup: Calling George after hearing his message
jokes.punchline: Missing the obvious point of answering machine messages
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about answering machine logic

jokes.text: But there is no way I'm gonna be a Suz.
jokes.punchline: Elaine's firm stand on name abbreviation
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Elaine's principles about identity

jokes.text: What am I? Some pom-pom waving, back-seat bimbo?
jokes.punchline: Specific stereotype associated with name
jokes.explanation: Good specific imagery - Elaine's class consciousness about names

jokes.text: No, I was kicked out for fighting with one of the players. / Wait. Wait. Wait. Who? / Reggie Miller.
jokes.setup: Kramer missing the game Jerry bet on
jokes.punchline: Escalating to fighting with professional athlete
jokes.explanation: Great Kramer escalation - casual mention of extraordinary event

jokes.text: Well, first of all, for some reason, they started the game an hour late.
jokes.setup: Explaining what happened at the game
jokes.punchline: Kramer's time change causing real consequences
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback - Kramer's earlier absurd decision has real consequences

jokes.text: Well, I ran out onto the court and threw a hot dog at Reggie Miller.
jokes.setup: Getting involved in Spike Lee's argument
jokes.punchline: Absurd escalation to food-throwing assault
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer moment - escalates conflict in most absurd way possible with perfect specificity

jokes.text: Then the three of us went to a strip club.
jokes.setup: Getting thrown out of game with Spike Lee and Reggie Miller
jokes.punchline: Bonding experience at strip club
jokes.explanation: Brilliant resolution - conflict becomes male bonding in most inappropriate way

jokes.text: I didn't know Cheryl Miller's little brother played basketball.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's oblivious gender reversal
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan absurdity - treating famous player as sibling footnote

jokes.text: See, that's a cool G, daddy-o. Now, you gotta let it ride.
jokes.punchline: Kramer's gambling slang and pressure to continue
jokes.explanation: Good Kramer characterization using gambling terminology

jokes.text: And between you, me and the lamppost, and the desk...
jokes.setup: Peterman trying to say 'between you and me'
jokes.punchline: Adding unnecessary objects to confidential phrase
jokes.explanation: Good Peterman malapropism - adding objects to idiom

jokes.text: I don't know, Mike, to me it sounds a little, how you say, phony.
jokes.setup: Mike can't pay the bet he took
jokes.punchline: Jerry using Mike's old insult against him
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff - Jerry turns Mike's insult back on him with perfect timing

jokes.text: So Allison and I think that the best thing to do is just make a clean break.
jokes.punchline: Kramer breaking up with George for Allison
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdity - proxy breakup with formal relationship language

jokes.text: I didn't even know you wanted to get serious. / So, what am I in this for?
jokes.punchline: George questioning his relationship with Kramer
jokes.explanation: Great absurd escalation - George treating friendship like romantic relationship

jokes.text: We don't have to name names or point fingers... or name names.
jokes.punchline: Redundant phrase repetition
jokes.explanation: Mild Peterman verbal confusion - repetitive phrasing

jokes.text: Me and her have had our problems. She and I have had our problems. You and I and she and you.
jokes.punchline: Grammatical confusion spiral
jokes.explanation: Good Peterman verbal confusion - grammar spiral creates humor

jokes.text: Elaine and Susie, Susie and Elaine.
jokes.punchline: Proposing meeting between same person twice
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - audience knows the impossibility

jokes.text: I'll get the money. I got a hundred in my pocket if you wanna reach in.
jokes.setup: Mike with broken thumbs owing money
jokes.punchline: Offering inappropriate physical solution
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor - inappropriate intimacy from desperation

jokes.text: Stupid thumbs.
jokes.punchline: Blaming his body parts
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy payoff with good economy

jokes.text: Oh, the trunk's broken. It's rattling.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's innocent explanation for trapped person
jokes.explanation: Dark humor - innocent explanation for criminal situation

jokes.text: Well, there's only one thing to do. Eliminate her. / What? / Get rid of Susie. Make her disappear.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sinister-sounding solution
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor - innocent advice sounds like murder plot

jokes.text: I've been walking around all night. I've been thinking about Allison and me and you.
jokes.punchline: Treating friendship breakup like romantic crisis
jokes.explanation: Great continuation of romantic relationship parody between friends

jokes.text: She took her own life.
jokes.punchline: Elaine's dramatic fake suicide announcement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - solving identity problem with fake death, perfect economy

jokes.text: We're taking George back. / What? / He's gonna make us very happy.
jokes.punchline: Treating friendship like romantic relationship
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of relationship comedy with friends

jokes.text: My real funeral's not gonna come close to this.
jokes.setup: Seeing large turnout for Susie's fake funeral
jokes.punchline: Elaine comparing to her own future funeral
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark observation - jealousy about own funeral attendance

jokes.text: I'm not Susie. I'm Elaine. / But I've been calling you Susie. / Hadn't noticed. Excuse me.
jokes.setup: Peggy realizing her mistake at funeral
jokes.punchline: Elaine's dismissive response to months of wrong name
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to identity confusion with casual dismissal

jokes.text: It's a rental, but I've had it for 15 years.
jokes.punchline: Impossibly long rental period
jokes.explanation: Perfect George logic - treating rental like ownership through time

jokes.text: And most of all, I will never forget that one night... working late on the catalog... just the two of us, and we surrendered to temptation. And it was pretty good.
jokes.punchline: Admitting sexual encounter with nonexistent person
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Peterman's delusion reaches sexual territory at funeral

jokes.text: Hold on! Susie didn't commit suicide. She was murdered... by Jerry Seinfeld!
jokes.setup: Mike interrupting funeral with broken thumbs
jokes.punchline: Accusing Jerry of murdering nonexistent person
jokes.explanation: Perfect Seinfeld escalation - absurd accusation at fake funeral creates chaos

jokes.text: I'm Susie. She's me. / I feel the same way.
jokes.setup: Elaine trying to reveal truth about Susie
jokes.punchline: Peterman misinterpreting as emotional connection
jokes.explanation: Good misunderstanding comedy - literal truth interpreted emotionally

jokes.text: Suz!
jokes.setup: Ending with Peterman calling Elaine by rejected nickname
jokes.punchline: Using the exact name she refused to accept
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending callback - Elaine gets stuck with worst possible nickname

tmdb_overview: Kramer becomes the spokesman for a woman George wants to avoid; Jerry's bookie welches.
